title: Evangelion 10 showing in San Francisco starting 9 11
display_title: Evangelion 10 showing in San Francisco starting 9 11
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 1.0 showing in San Francisco starting 9/11!!             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Aug 19, 2009 11:51 pm http://www.evageeks.org/2009/08/evangelion-1-0-showing-in-san-francisco/ YAY! Dubbed or subbed I don't care, I am SO there!

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Wed Aug 19, 2009 11:55 pm Cant make the time or money to fly down to LA just to see a movie, even if that movie's Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Aug 19, 2009 11:59 pm LA is south California, San Francisco is the Bay Area.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Thu Aug 20, 2009 12:01 am California then. Im just saying I cant make it to the theaters. :( Ill try to get ahold of the dvd.

[]:             Quote Post by roblucci01 » Thu Aug 20, 2009 1:42 pm Crossing my fingers for the film to hit Florida.

[]:             Quote Post by Evafan » Thu Aug 20, 2009 3:31 pm I might find some time for this hopefully. Too bad it wasn't 2 weeks earlier, since school is starting the 24th

[]:             Quote Post by Typhon » Thu Aug 20, 2009 3:41 pm They spelled Evangelion wrong........Wrong I say!!

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Thu Aug 20, 2009 7:14 pm :cries: All 1.0 showings are very far away from me.

[]:             Quote Post by CZR » Thu Aug 20, 2009 8:36 pm I'm going to the showing in Portland tomorrow. Can't wait. Also the showing here is listed as the English dub for those curious. Gotta throw my NERV logo shirt in the wash tonight. :)

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Fri Aug 21, 2009 12:40 am Well it comes round to my town of Oakville over here in Canada in september so Im excited for that.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Fri Aug 21, 2009 9:05 pm I saw it today on the big screen (in Portland)! It was awesome! Visuals: These were incredibly good. Like, they left everything from the 1995 version in the dust. That tetrahedron angel-thingie was so beautiful compared to the original one, i was almost sad when they killed it (although that part looked awesome). Audio: The dub wasn't bad as far as dubs go but nevertheless there where some parts where I lol'ed at the word choice. I don't like dubs. Music: Awesome, though not as stunning as the visuals. The changes were, IMO, all for the better and they did a really good job with everything. I was blown away. I'm now scared that the original won't look as good in the face of this new power. This movie totally made my day.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Sat Aug 22, 2009 12:16 am schismatics wrote: :cries: All 1.0 showings are very far away from me.

[]:             Quote Post by trueno_twentyone » Sat Aug 22, 2009 3:52 am Why can't eva make it here in the Philippines! dammit!

[]:             Quote Post by Smothmoth » Wed Sep 02, 2009 1:18 am I just got an email from the Kabuki Theater, the showing has been moved back to September 11th. Also, seeing as the showing is listed on Funimation's events, I bet it's the dub.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Wed Sep 02, 2009 9:31 am *pats trueno 's shoulder* rest assured, it will make it in the philippines before it does here in panama.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Sep 02, 2009 11:23 am Ba-bump. http://www.evageeks.org/2009/08/evangelion-1-0-showing-in-san-francisco/ It turns out the date has been changed to 9/11 for the showing. I have no idea if it WILL be a weekly run or not as advertised on the postcard though. Once I find out if it'll be dubbed or subtitled, I'll update with that information too.

[]:             Quote Post by wonderluster » Wed Sep 02, 2009 5:59 pm You know I always feel kinda freaked out when movies are released on 9/11. This one especially. I mean like, giant robots? ( Don't nag me bout the synthetics, I get it )

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed Sep 02, 2009 9:55 pm I do think the fact that a post-apocalyptic film is showing on September 11 shows how long it's been since that Tuesday morning in 2001 even in San Francisco (sorry to stereotype, but it's too true IMO), I could not imagine this being shown back in 2002 or 2003, especially how you had that really bizarre list of "banned songs" that couldn't be played on the radio for a time because they supposedly invoked too much of some aspect of 9/11

[]:             Quote Post by squall.leonhart » Wed Sep 02, 2009 11:56 pm I hope to Yui... err, I mean, God Almighty it will be a weekly run so I can see it on Sunday on my day off. Already makin' plans.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Sep 03, 2009 12:11 am I'll try to see if I can make it, but I doubt my mother will pick me up from school and drive me two hours to San Francisco after a long day of work just for something that only I'll enjoy. ^^;
